seo.title: West Virginia CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to West Virginia - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity west virginia, how to transfer CNA license to west virginia, west virginia CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in West Virginia.

steps.title: Complete a State-Approved Training Program
steps.description: Finish an West Virginia-approved CNA training program, usually 120 hours long.

steps.title: Pass the Competency Exam
steps.description: Pass both the written and clinical skills portions of the West Virginia nurse aide exam.

steps.title: Submit Your Application
steps.description: Apply for certification, complete any required background check steps, and pay the required fees.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does West Virginia CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: There is no application fee for CNA endorsement in West Virginia. However, background check fees may apply.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: No, fingerprints are generally not required for CNA endorsement in West Virginia.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to West Virginia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in West Virginia while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in WV.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to West Virginia.

hero.title: Become a CNA in West Virginia
hero.dek: West Virginia CNA certification requires a 120-hour training program, West Virginia DHHR eligibility, and a passing score on the NNAAP (Prometric) exam.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in West Virginia
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to West Virginia CNA certification.
